\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Provides methods for various mesh-\/related operations. 

This class includes static methods for computing geometric properties of mesh elements, such as the area of triangles, the length of edges, and the normal vectors of edges. 

Definition at line 111 of file Arcane\+Fem\+Functions.\+h.

\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{computeAreaQuad4()}{computeAreaQuad4()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily static Real Arcane\+Fem\+Functions\+::\+Mesh\+Operation\+::compute\+Area\+Quad4 (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{Item\+With\+Nodes}]{item,  }\item[{const Variable\+Node\+Real3 \&}]{node\+\_\+coord }\end{DoxyParamCaption})\hspace{0.3cm}{\ttfamily [inline]}, {\ttfamily [static]}}



Computes the area of a quadrilateral defined by four nodes. 

This method calculates the area of a quadrilateral by breaking it down into two triangles and using the determinant formula. The area is computed as half the value of the determinant of the matrix formed by the coordinates of the quadrilateral\textquotesingle{}s vertices.

Computes the area of a triangle defined by three nodes. 

This method calculates the area using the determinant formula for a triangle. The area is computed as half the value of the determinant of the matrix formed by the coordinates of the triangle\textquotesingle{}s vertices.

Computes the barycenter (centroid) of a tetrahedron. 

This method calculates the barycenter of a tetrahedron defined by its nodes. The barycenter is computed as the average of the vertices\textquotesingle{} coordinates.

Computes the normalized quad normal for a given face. 

This method calculates normal vector to the quad defined by nodes, of the face and normalizes it, and ensures the correct orientation. Newell\textquotesingle{}s Method is used to compute the normal vector for the quadrilateral.

{\footnotesize\ttfamily static Real Arcane\+Fem\+Functions\+::\+Mesh\+Operation\+::compute\+Volume\+Tetra4 (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{Item\+With\+Nodes}]{item,  }\item[{const Variable\+Node\+Real3 \&}]{node\+\_\+coord }\end{DoxyParamCaption})\hspace{0.3cm}{\ttfamily [inline]}, {\ttfamily [static]}}



Computes the volume of a tetrahedra defined by four nodes. 

This method calculates the volume using the scalar triple product formula. We do the following\+:
\begin{DoxyEnumerate}
\item get the four nodes
\item ge the vector representing the edges of tetrahedron
\item compute volume using scalar triple product 
\end{DoxyEnumerate}
